channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hundreds of thousands rally in Istanbul in support of imprisoned mayor
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of protesters again congregated in Istanbul Saturday to how their support for the city's imprisoned mayor and demand his release.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Doctor cites the pope's 'surprising improvement' after surviving life-threatening crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has shown ‘’a truly surprising improvement’’ since returning to the Vatican to convalesce after surviving a life-threatening bout with double-pneumonia, the doctor who coordinated the pontiff’s five-week hospitalization said Saturday. “I find him very lively,’’ Dr.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kalshi CEO: Political betting could be ‘a trillion dollar’ market
item.description: Kalshi says it’s the first regulated exchange where people “can buy and sell contracts on the outcome of events.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, The Hill

item.title: Nostalgia, relief and loss as some Syrians mark their first Ramadan back home in years
item.description: DARAYA, Syria (AP) — When Mariam Aabour learned of the ouster of Syrian leader Bashar Assad, she shed tears of joy. But as the time came to return to her homeland from Lebanon – where she fled years earlier – Aabour felt torn.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Danish foreign minister scolds Trump administration for its criticism of Denmark and Greenland
item.description: The Danish foreign minister has scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Philip Crowther, Kirsten Grieshaber And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump effect forces Germany to reprioritize defense as nation plays catch-up in military spending
item.description: In his second term as president, Donald Trump is pushing American allies, including Germany, to do more to fund their militaries and pay their fair share to fund defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's Signal scandal fuels growing distrust from allies
item.description: The Trump administration’s sharing of sensitive military information on Signal has shocked allies, while further eroding trans-Atlantic trust thanks to group chat messages from top U.S. officials expressing contempt toward Europe's “freeloading” on security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: New US strikes against Houthi rebels kill at least 1 in Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pounded Yemen overnight into Saturday, reportedly killing at least one person as the American military acknowledged earlier bombing a major military site in the heart of Sanaa controlled by the Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military has said on state television that the confirmed death toll from a devastating earthquake rose to1,644.
item.dc:creator: David Rising And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Massive quake rocks Myanmar and Thailand. Hundreds feared dead
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A powerful earthquake rocked Myanmar on Friday, causing extensive damage across a wide swath of one of the world’s poorest countries and prompting officials to warn that the initial death toll — above 140 — was likely to grow in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war
item.description: Israel has launched an attack on Beirut, marking its first strike on Lebanon's capital since a ceasefire ended the latest Israel-Hezbollah war last November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Major earthquakes strike Myanmar and Thailand
item.description: BANGKOK, Thailand (AP) — A 7.7 magnitude earthquake and an aftershock measuring 6.4 rocked Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, destroying buildings, a bridge and a dam. At least 144 people were killed in Myanmar, the head of the military government said in the televised speech.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel launched an attack on the Lebanese capital Beirut on Friday for the first time since a ceasefire ended fighting between Israeli forces and the Hezbollah militant group in November.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Denmark in Trump's crosshairs as Vance makes ambitious appeal to Greenland
item.description: Vice President Vance looked to appeal to Greenland by urging it to side with the US, not Russia or China, after it seeks independence from Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, The Hill

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance says Denmark has “underinvested” in Greenland’s security and demands Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump continues to talk of taking over the Danish territory.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grieshaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rubio warns visas will be revoked for all foreign student ‘activists’ amid Tufts arrest
item.description: Rubio says student visas are for studying, “not to become a social activist," and warns they’ll be revoked if foreigners are seen as engaging in “destabilizing" acts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Europe says no to Trump push to lift sanctions on Russia, upending Black Sea ceasefire
item.description: Europe says no to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure some sanction relief for Russia as he tries to make a Black Sea ceasefire deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human Rights Council blocks watchdog chief from criticizing UN official accused of antisemitism
item.description: The U.N. Human Rights Council blocked Hillel Neuer from finishing his remarks criticizing Special Rapporteur for the Occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Signal leak: Trump adviser Mike Waltz may have to go, Warner says
item.description: “There needs to be accountability,” said Sen. Warner, who faults Trump's inner circle with a number of security gaffes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WGN 9

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director attacked by Israeli settlers, detained by army
item.description: Co-director Basel Adra witnessed the detention and said around two dozen settlers — some masked, some carrying guns, some in Israeli uniform — attacked the village.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A Russian drone attack kills 7 in Ukraine ahead of ceasefire talks
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a barrage of drones across Ukraine overnight Sunday that killed at least seven people, according to local Ukrainian officials and emergency services.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation
